zh.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley於1038年出生在法國諾門迪。他和Emaline Saer至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他於1075年登記服兵役。他去世於1085年3月27日在英國賀福爾賀福德享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley于1038年出生在法国諾門迪。他和Emaline Saer至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。他于1075年登记服兵役。他去世于1085年3月27日在英国賀福爾賀福德享年47岁，埋葬在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾格盧斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: Weobley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German   Weible  .

variants.name: Weible
meanings: German:
from the personal name Wiibo, a short form of a compound name formed with wīg ‘battle, fight’ as the first element + a second element beginning with b, such as berht ‘bright’ or bald ‘bold’.
variant of   Weibel  .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Attainment’.

nameMeanings.name: Lacy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, originally a Norman baronial name from Lassy, Calvados. The Lacey family was powerful in Ireland during the early Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

en.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley was born in 1038, in Normandy, France. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Emaline Saer. He registered for military service in 1075. He died on 27 March 1085, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley est né en 1038, à Normandie, France. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Emaline Saer. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1075. Il est décédé le 27 mars 1085, à Hereford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley wurde 1038 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mit Emaline Saer mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1075 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 27. März 1085 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Hereford, Herefordshire, England und wurde in Gloucester, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley è nato il nel 1038 nel Normandie, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Emaline Saer. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1075. Morì il 27 marzo 1085, nel Hereford, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobleyは1038年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた。彼はEmaline Saerとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1075年に兵役登録をした。彼は1085年3月27日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアハーエフォードで47歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアグロウセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley(은)는 1038년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났다. 그는 Emaline Saer(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1075년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1085년 3월 27일 영국 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 그루스스터에 묻혔다.

mn.Chapter House, Gloucester Abbey, Glouces: Gloucester, Glevum, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley nasceu em 1038, em Normandie, França. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Emaline Saer. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1075. Ele faleceu em 27 de março de 1085, em Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley родился в 1038 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Emaline Saer. В 1075 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 27 марта 1085 г. в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности глоусестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Walter de Lacy Baron Weobley nació en 1038, en Normandie, Francia. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Emaline Saer. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1075. Murió el 27 de marzo de 1085, en Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultado en Gloucester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
